target.name: Skydio
target.founder: Adam Bry, Abe Bachrach, Matt Donahoe

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $4.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 15 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $18.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Skydio, a Series F autonomous systems company in Defense & Security, commands a current post-money valuation of $4.4B following $825M+ in cumulative capital raised. The valuation is triangulated against a comp universe of 15 private peers in the same sector and valuation tier, supplemented by two public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). Representative private peers include Helsing ($18.0B, Series D), Shield AI ($12.7B, Series G), and Saronic ($9.2B, Series D), with peer valuations ranging $1.0B to $18.0B and a median of $2.2B.

Two valuation methods apply. Stage-progression modeling applies a 1.3x step-up multiplier to the $4.4B last post-money, implying $5.7B at the next financing stage. Peer-median analysis across the 15-company Defense & Security comp set yields $2.2B, reflecting the wide dispersion in the peer universe ($1.0B–$18.0B range). The divergence reflects heterogeneity in stage maturity and commercialization progress across peers.

Triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $1.9B (low) to $6.6B (high), with a median of $4.0B. The current $4.4B valuation sits modestly above the computed median, within the interquartile range. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, customer concentration in federal/allied defense procurement, and regulatory classification changes affecting export eligibility.
